problem: A triangle is made of wood sticks of lengths 8, 15 and 17 inches joined end-to-end. Pieces of the same integral length are cut from each of the sticks so that the three remaining pieces can no longer form a triangle. How many inches are in the length of the smallest piece that can be cut from each of the three sticks to make this happen?

classified_questions.question: Can you describe the initial triangle made of wood sticks with lengths 8, 15, and 17 inches?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: How did you determine that pieces of the same integral length need to be cut from each of the sticks?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: How did you come up with the equation $(8-x)+(15-x) \leq (17-x)$ to determine when the three remaining pieces can no longer form a triangle?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Why is it important that the length of the pieces cut from each of the sticks is an integer?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Can you think of any other similar problems where finding the smallest possible piece or length is important?
classified_questions.label: e
